channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenya Journalist Escapes Abduction After President's Criticism of Media Outlet
item.description: A senior journalist at The Standard Group, Alex Kiprotich, narrowly escaped an attempted abduction by armed men days after President William Ruto criticized his media outlet. The Committee to Protect ...

item.title: Oil Prices Rise Amid U.S.-Iran Tensions
item.description: Oil prices jumped over 5% as President Donald Trump declared the interim agreement with Iran on peace was over. The U.S. strikes on Iran followed attacks on three ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Brent ...

item.title: Celebrating the Gum Tree: An Exhibition at Melbourne’s Potter Museum
item.description: A new exhibition titled 'Ngarn Wa’ngal' at Melbourne’s Potter Museum of Art showcases the cultural and ecological significance of the eucalyptus tree, known as 'gumtree' in Australian English. The exh...

item.title: Amit Shah reviews heavy rain situation with Maharashtra, Gujarat and Jammu & Kashmir chief ministers, assures Central assistance
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah spoke separately with the Chief Ministers of Maharashtra, Gujarat and Jammu & Kashmir to review the situation arising from heavy rainfall affecting these regions. During ...

item.title: South African Soccer Teams Embark on Pre-Season European Tours Ahead of New Season
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs, Orlando Pirates, and Mamelodi Sundowns are preparing for the new Betway Premiership season by participating in high-profile pre-season matches across Europe. Chiefs will train in Murcia...

item.title: Balochistan Attacks Result in Casualties; ISPR DG Commits to Hunt Down Terrorists
item.description: The Director General of Inter-Services Public Relations (ISPR), Lt Gen Ahmed Sharif Chaudhry, addressed a press conference detailing recent terrorist attacks in Balochistan. Over the past four days, t...

item.title: Two Policemen Killed in Ambush in Sultan Kudarat
item.description: Two policemen, Patrolman Apollo Morante Pagurayan and Patrolman Joerel Basilan Bullos, were killed in an ambush in Barangay Tinumigues, Lambayong, Sultan Kudarat province. The incident occurred on Jul...

item.title: Call for Stricter Gambling Ad Regulations as Government Faces Criticism
item.description: The article highlights the growing divide between public demand and government action regarding gambling advertising restrictions. Critics argue that the Albanese government's proposed legislation, wh...

item.title: Lobby Group Sues Speakers Wetang'ula and Kingi for Campaigning During Tenure
item.description: A lobby group has filed a petition at the High Court seeking to bar National Assembly speaker Moses Wetang'ula and Senate speaker Amason Kingi from participating in partisan political campaigns while ...

item.title: Senator Marcoleta and Former Congressman Defensor Arrested Over Alleged Gift Prohibition Violations
item.description: The Sandiganbayan's Fourth Division has ordered the arrest of Senator Rodante Marcoleta and former congressman Mike Defensor for allegedly violating Presidential Decree No. 46, which prohibits public ...

item.title: Farage triggers Clacton by-election amid political uncertainty
item.description: Nigel Farage has initiated a by-election in his Essex constituency, Clacton, despite opposition from major parties. The move comes as Count Binface, a satirical candidate representing the Monster Ravi...

item.title: India's Government Announces Major Policy Reforms for Sustainable Development
item.description: The article discusses the Indian government's recent initiatives aimed at promoting sustainable development and economic growth. Key policies include investments in renewable energy, infrastructure de...

item.title: Ministers Oversee Enhancements for Airport Passenger Experience
item.description: Home Minister Amit Shah and Civil Aviation Minister Ram Mohan Naidu Kinjarapu reviewed passenger facilities, security arrangements, and immigration systems at major airports. The meeting, attended by ...

item.title: Balochistan Attacks Result in 42 Martyrs and 54 Terrorists Neutralized
item.description: The DG ISPR reported three major terrorist incidents in Balochistan over four days, resulting in the martyrdom of 42 security personnel and neutralization of 54 terrorists. The first attack on July 4/...

item.title: Edwin Sifuna's Rising Popularity as a Potential Ruto Challenger in Kenya's 2027 Election
item.description: The article discusses the growing political influence of Edwin Sifuna, a Nairobi senator, within Kenya's opposition. Sifuna's support among Gen Z voters is seen as a potential threat to President Will...

item.title: Adesuwa Okunbo-Rhodes' journey from investment banking to founding Aruwa Capital and addressing Nigeria's SME funding gap
item.description: Adesuwa Okunbo-Rhodes, who started her global banking career at 18, now drives impactful investments as head of Aruwa Capital. She began her finance career at Lehman Brothers, later moving to J.P. Mor...

item.title: Arthur Fery's Wimbledon Triumph: A British Hopeful's Historic Run
item.description: Arthur Fery, a 23-year-old British tennis player ranked 114th in the world, has defied expectations by reaching the Wimbledon singles quarter-finals as a wildcard entry. His unexpected victory over fo...

item.title: Kerala Debris Slip: Search Continues for Five Missing After Tunnel Construction Site Collapse
item.description: A deadly debris slip occurred at a tunnel road construction site in Wayanad district of Kerala, India, on July 7, 2026, resulting in three fatalities and leaving five people missing. Rescue operations...

item.title: Constitutional Court's NHI Ruling and Its Impact on South Africa's Health Policy
item.description: The article discusses the significance of the Constitutional Court's ruling on South Africa's National Health Insurance (NHI) legislation, emphasizing its role in ensuring proper legislative processes...

item.title: University of Nairobi Graduate Struggles with Job Hunt After 11 Years
item.description: Steve Muikia, a former banker and University of Nairobi Economics graduate, has spent over a decade searching for work without success. Pushed to the wall by mounting debts and unmet basic needs, Muik...
